title: alexitoxicon laxum
intro: Alexitoxicon laxum, also known as poison gland or poison plant, is a genus of flowering plants in the family Boraginaceae, native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Alexitoxicon laxum include relief from muscle spasms, pain, and inflammation, as well as support for the digestive system and immune system. Herbal preparations of Alexitoxicon laxum can be made into teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to treat a variety of health issues.
